mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly supporting higher S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.96 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during late-cycle phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in expansionary regime windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 236.0 indicate persistent inflationary pressure that forces markets to price in tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 236.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of economic resilience and wage-driven consumption, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS levels falls below 0.03, or 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates synchronized demand across production and consumption channels, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative or insignificant coefficients in out-of-sample return regressions over 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing output, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days during economic slowdown phases.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during slowdown regimes, with hit rate below 45% in out-of-sample validation.
